[https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/hugo-salabio/summary.html Rugby League Project] | updated = {{date|{{REVISIONTIMESTAMP}}|DMY}} }} '''Hugo Salabio''' (born 27 July 2000) is a French professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} forward for [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on short-term loan from [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]] and {{rlnt|France}} at international level. He has previously played for the [[Catalans Dragons]], [[Wakefield Trinity]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]] and for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. ==Playing career== ===Catalans Dragons=== In 2022, he made his [[Catalans Dragons|Catalans]] debut in the [[Super League]] against the [[Wigan Warriors]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62774591|title=Super League: Wigan Warriors 48-4 Catalans Dragons - Warriors claim eight tries to finish second|publisher=BBC|date=2 September 2022|accessdate=3 September 2022}} ===Pia Donkeys=== He joined [[Pia Donkeys|Pia]] in the [[Elite One Championship]] before joining Wakefield Trinity in June 2023.{{citation needed|date=March 2024}} ===Wakefield Trinity=== In round 15 of the 2023 Super League season, on his Wakefield debut Salabio was given a red card for a dangerous lifting tackle on Leeds player [[Richie Myler]] during Wakefield Trinity's 24-14 victory. It was Wakefield's first victory of the campaign having lost their opening 14 matches.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65822026|title=Super League: Wakefield 24-14 Leeds - Bottom club chalk up first win|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} On 13 June 2023, Salabio was suspended for seven games over the incident and fined £1,000.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65898176|title=Hugo Salabio: Wakefield Trinity trialist prop banned for seven games after spear tackle|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} ===Huddersfield Giants=== On 12 October 2023, it was reported that he had signed with [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] for the 2024 season on a three-year deal.{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=12 Oct 2023|title=Hugo Salabio: Huddersfield Giants sign forward on three-year deal from Wakefield Trinity|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67090290#:~:text=Huddersfield%20Giants%20have%20signed%20forward,which%20came%20from%20the%20bench.}} ===Hull F.C.=== On 22 December 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull F.C.]] in the Super League on a two-year deal.{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=22 Dec 2024|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Hull FC formally announce signing of forward days after Huddersfield Giants exit|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-fc-formally-announce-signing-of-forward-days-after-huddersfield-giants-exit}} ==== Doncaster RLFC (DR) ==== On 21 March 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on [[RFL dual registration|DR loan]]{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=21 Mar 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Newly-signed Hull FC forward makes Championship switch|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/newly-signed-hull-fc-forward-makes-championship-switch}} ==== Castleford Tigers (loan) ==== On 15 April 2025, [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Salabio on a season-long loan deal, with Hull holding a two-week recall option.{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=15 April 2025 |title=Hull FC prop makes season-long loan move to Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-fc-prop-makes-season-long-loan-move-to-castleford-tigers/ |access-date=15 April 2025 |website=TotalRL}} He made his Castleford debut on 17 April 2025 in the Round 8 game against [[Wakefield Trinity]]. Salabio made five appearances for the Tigers before he was recalled by Hull FC on 9 September 2025.{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=9 September 2025 |title=Hull FC recall forward from Super League loan amid pending Jack Ashworth ban |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-fc-recall-forward-from-super-league-loan-amid-pending-jack-ashworth-ban |access-date=11 September 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}} ====Halifax Panthers (loan)==== On 6 March 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on short-term loan{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=6 Mar 2026|accessdate=7 Mar 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Halifax Panthers secure loan additions from Hull FC and Wakefield Trinity|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/halifax-panthers-hull-fc-wakefield-trinity}} ==References== {{reflist|30em}} ==External links== *[http://www.catalansdragons.com/en/fiche-665-salabio-hugo/ Catalans Dragons profile] {{Halifax Panthers squad}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Salabio, Hugo}} [[Category:2000 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:21st-century French sportsmen]] [[Category:AS Saint Estève players]] [[Category:Baroudeurs de Pia XIII players]] [[Category:Castleford Tigers players]] [[Category:Catalans Dragons players]] [[Category:Doncaster R.L.F.C. players]] [[Category:France national rugby league team players]] [[Category:French rugby league players]] [[Category:Halifax Panthers players]] [[Category:Huddersfield Giants players]] [[Category:Hull F.C. players]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Marseille]] [[Category:Rugby league props]] [[Category:Toulouse Olympique players]] [[Category:Wakefield Trinity players]] {{France-rugbyleague-bio-stub}}
